title: Greg McCortney: Abortion Rights Man of the Year
author: James Silberman
orientation: Free the States names Oklahoma State Senator Greg McCortney the 2020 'Abortion Rights Man of the Year' for blocking SB13 (the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act) from a committee hearing, voting to kill it on the Senate floor, and publicly slandering abolitionists as a security threat. The article frames McCortney as the single biggest obstacle to abolishing abortion in Oklahoma in 2020 and ties his actions to a National Right to Life endorsement.
audiences: Oklahoma voter in McCortney's district weighing the 2020 primary
Self-identified pro-life Christian unfamiliar with the abolitionist critique
Journalist or political reporter covering the SB13 fight
Pastor or church leader navigating Right to Life affiliations

questions.question: How did Greg McCortney end up with sole authority over whether SB13 received a hearing?
questions.audience: Journalist or political reporter covering the SB13 fight
questions.answer: After Senator Jason Smalley abruptly resigned, Senate President Pro Tem Greg Treat appointed McCortney to chair the Senate Health and Human Services committee on January 27, 2020, which gave him unilateral discretion over whether the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act would be heard.
questions.quote: In the wake of Jason Smalley's abrupt resignation from the State Senate, Greg McCortney was announced as the new chair of the Senate Health and Human Services (HHS) committee on January 27. His new position came with sole discretion in deciding whether to hear the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act (SB13).

questions.question: What did Free the States actually do to try to persuade McCortney before criticizing him?
questions.audience: Self-identified pro-life Christian unfamiliar with the abolitionist critique
questions.answer: They explicitly extended benefit of the doubt, organized a respectful email campaign to his office requesting a hearing for SB13, and canvassed his district with door hangers framing him as a potential hero for preborn children rather than an enemy.
questions.quote: We asked people to [kindly but firmly email McCortney](https://www.facebook.com/FreeTheStates/photos/a.451653001933823/861450857620700/?type=3&theaterey&epa=SEARCH_BOX) asking for SB13 to be given a hearing. We covered his district with door hangers letting people know that McCortney was in the position to be a hero for preborn children.

questions.question: What were the specific committee dates McCortney could have used to hear SB13, and what did he do instead?
questions.answer: The committee deadline was February 27 with available hearing slots on February 17 and 24. McCortney let February 17 pass without action and on February 21 announced SB13 would not be heard on February 24 either, effectively killing the bill in committee.
questions.quote: The deadline for passing a bill out of committee was February 27 and there were committee hearings where SB13 could have been heard on February 17 and 24. It was not heard on February 17 and on February 21, McCortney [announced that it would not be heard on February 24 either](https://freethestates.org/2020/02/senator-mccortney-again-denies-sb13-a-fair-hearing/index.html). SB13 had been denied a committee hearing.

questions.question: After the committee deadline passed, was there any procedural path left to revive SB13?
questions.audience: Journalist or political reporter covering the SB13 fight
questions.answer: Yes. Senate Rule 7-9 allows a bill to be withdrawn from committee and placed on General Order so it can be brought to the floor. On March 11, primary author Senator Joseph Silk made that motion, but Senator Kim David counter-motioned to table it, McCortney voted yea on her counter-motion, and the bill died.
questions.quote: Senate Rule 7-9 allows for legislation to be withdrawn from committee and placed on General Order where it can then be brought to the Senate floor for a hearing. On March 11, Senator Joseph Silk, primary author of the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act, motioned to do exactly that. Kim David, who is the [Abortion Rights Woman of the Year](https://www.facebook.com/search/top/?q=abortion%20rights%20woman%20of%20the%20year&epa=SEARCH_BOX), made a [counter-motion to table Silk's motion](https://www.facebook.com/watch/?v=549375959015145) and thereby kill SB13 for good. McCortney voted 'Yea' on David's counter-motion. SB13 died.

questions.question: What did McCortney publicly say about abolitionists on the day of the floor vote?
questions.audience: Pastor or church leader navigating Right to Life affiliations
questions.answer: He tweeted that the Oklahoma Highway Patrol was present 'in numbers' and that there had been 'multiple security briefings' because Christian abolitionists were returning to the capitol, calling their presence an embarrassment to Christianity, despite the article's claim that abolitionists had never caused a single security incident.
questions.quote: McCortney dishonestly tweeted that abolitionists were an embarrassment to Christianity because of how much of a security threat we are, knowing full well that there has never been a single security threat caused by abolitionists: "The [@OHPtraffic](https://twitter.com/OHPtraffic) is here in numbers and there have been multiple security briefings because the Christians are coming back to [#OKLeg](https://twitter.com/hashtag/OKLeg?src=hashtag_click) today. That statement is embarrassing."

questions.question: Why does the article treat McCortney's portrayal of abolitionists as part of a larger pattern rather than a one-off comment?
questions.answer: The piece argues that pro-life senators have repeatedly tried to paint abolitionists as unhinged and dangerous specifically to delegitimize the abolition bill and the broader movement, and McCortney's tweet is presented as one more instance of that recurring discrediting tactic.
questions.quote: This has been a recurring tactic among the pro-life Senators who want to falsely portray abolitionists as crazy and violent in an attempt to discredit the abolition bill and the Abolitionist Movement.

questions.question: What does an endorsement from National Right to Life signal about a candidate, according to Free the States?
questions.audience: Pastor or church leader navigating Right to Life affiliations
questions.answer: Free the States argues NRTL is the premiere anti-abolition lobby in states where pro-lifers control government, citing its opposition in Indiana, Idaho, and Oklahoma, so accepting Tony Lauinger's endorsement is treated as a clear marker that a candidate opposes immediate abolition.
questions.quote: National Right to Life is one of the premiere anti-abolition organizations. In states where pro-lifers control the state government and pro-choicers don't have the political power to effectively fight an abolition bill, Right to Life is the organization lobbying against abolishing abortion. Receiving their endorsement is a clear indication of someone who opposes abortion's immediate abolition.

questions.question: How does the article connect Tony Lauinger's candidate survey to the broader anti-abolition strategy?
questions.audience: Pastor or church leader navigating Right to Life affiliations
questions.answer: It claims the Oklahomans for Life candidate survey is worded to covertly extract opposition to the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act from candidates, framing the survey as a vetting tool used to lock in anti-abolition votes under a pro-life label.
questions.quote: Lauinger's candidate survey here in Oklahoma [slyly asks candidates to oppose the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act](https://freethestates.org/2020/05/oklahomans-for-life-candidates-oppose-abolition/index.html).

questions.question: What scriptural argument did abolitionists publicly aim at McCortney after his security tweet?
questions.audience: Self-identified pro-life Christian unfamiliar with the abolitionist critique
questions.answer: Chris Gore quoted Proverbs 17:15 directly at McCortney, framing his defense of an abortion-permitting status quo as 'justifying the wicked' and condemning the righteous, both of which the verse calls an abomination to the Lord.
questions.quote:
Proverbs 17:15  
He who justifies the wicked and he who condemns the righteous  
are both alike an abomination to the Lord.

questions.question: Is there an electoral alternative to McCortney that voters in his district can support in 2020?
questions.audience: Oklahoma voter in McCortney's district weighing the 2020 primary
questions.answer: Yes. The article points readers to Carisa Roberson, who is mounting a primary challenge against McCortney and has publicly committed to supporting the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act.
questions.quote: The good news is that McCortney is currently facing a primary challenge from [Carisa Roberson](https://www.voteroberson2020.com/), an abolitionist who has promised to support the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act.

questions.question: What is the article's overall verdict on McCortney's role in the 2020 fight to abolish abortion in Oklahoma?
questions.audience: Journalist or political reporter covering the SB13 fight
questions.answer: Free the States argues that across every procedural opportunity, including the committee chairmanship and the floor motion, McCortney chose obstruction, and that no other individual did more to keep abortion legal in Oklahoma in 2020.
questions.quote: At every turn, McCortney was there to obstruct the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act. He did more than anyone else to keep the murder of preborn children legal in Oklahoma in 2020.

related.title: Senator McCortney Again Denies SB13 a Fair Hearing
related.reason: The contemporaneous Free the States report from February 2020 documenting McCortney's second refusal to grant SB13 a committee hearing, which this article references directly.

related.title: National Right to Life Endorses Greg McCortney, the Senator Who Kept Abortion Legal in Oklahoma
related.reason: Expands on the Tony Lauinger / National Right to Life endorsement that the 'Man of the Year' piece treats as confirmation of McCortney's anti-abolition posture.

related.title: My Conversation With Senator Greg McCortney
related.reason: Provides a first-person account of dialogue with McCortney about SB13, useful context for readers wanting to evaluate the senator's reasoning beyond the public tweets.

related.title: Motion to Bring SB13 to Senate Floor Coming Tomorrow! Email Your Senator!
related.reason: Sets up the March 11 Senate Rule 7-9 floor motion by Joseph Silk that this article describes McCortney voting to table.

related.title: Free the States Statement on Sen. Jason Smalley's Resignation
related.reason: Covers the Smalley resignation that opened the HHS committee chair seat Greg Treat then handed to McCortney.

related.title: The Inexhaustible Cowardice of Oklahoma Senate President Greg Treat
related.reason: Profiles the Senate leader who appointed McCortney to the chairmanship, providing the leadership context behind the procedural gatekeeping described here.

related.title: Why "No Bill But SB13"?
related.reason: Explains the strategic and moral case for the specific legislation McCortney blocked, useful for readers new to why SB13 mattered.
